name: git-pushing
description: Stage, commit, and push git changes with conventional commit messages. Use when user wants to commit and push changes, mentions pushing to remote, or asks to save and push their work. Also activates when user says "push changes", "commit and push", "push this", "push to github", or similar git workflow requests.
capabilities: Stage all changed files automatically with git add
Push changes to remote repository with upstream tracking
Support custom commit messages via parameter
author: sickn33
securitySummary: All static analysis findings were evaluated and determined to be false positives. The shell script uses standard git commands with hardcoded arguments. Documentation examples in SKILL.md are markdown code blocks, not executable code. No security risks identified.
valueStatement: Streamline your git workflow by automating staging, conventional commits, and pushing with upstream tracking. Eliminate repetitive git commands with a single skill invocation.
userTitle: Automate Git Commit and Push Workflow
limitations: Requires git repository to be initialized
Requires remote origin to be configured for push operations
Does not handle merge conflicts automatically
Requires authentication credentials to be pre-configured

useCases.targetUser: Developers who frequently backup work to remote
useCases.title: Quick Save and Push
useCases.description: Save work-in-progress changes to remote repository with a single command

useCases.targetUser: Software engineers following conventional commit standards
useCases.title: Feature Completion Workflow
useCases.description: Commit and push completed features with proper conventional commit format

useCases.targetUser: Teams using AI coding assistants for development
useCases.title: Automated Git Operations
useCases.description: Integrate git operations into AI-assisted development workflows

promptTemplates.title: Basic Push
promptTemplates.prompt: Push my current changes to the remote repository
promptTemplates.scenario: User wants to quickly save and push work

promptTemplates.title: Push with Custom Message
promptTemplates.prompt: Commit and push with message: feat: add user authentication
promptTemplates.scenario: User wants to specify a conventional commit message

promptTemplates.title: Save Work to GitHub
promptTemplates.prompt: Save these changes and push them to GitHub
promptTemplates.scenario: User wants to backup work to remote

promptTemplates.title: Complete Feature Push
promptTemplates.prompt: I finished the login feature. Commit with proper message and push to remote
promptTemplates.scenario: User completed a feature and wants to share it

bestPractices: Review staged changes before pushing to ensure no sensitive files are included
Use meaningful conventional commit messages that describe the change purpose
Verify remote repository access and authentication before initiating push operations
antiPatterns: Do not push commits containing hardcoded credentials or API keys
Avoid pushing large binary files or build artifacts to the repository
Do not use this skill when working with sensitive or classified code repositories

faq.question: What happens if I have untracked files?
faq.answer: The skill stages all changes including untracked files using git add. Review your changes before running to ensure nothing unintended is included.

faq.question: Can I specify a custom branch to push to?
faq.answer: The skill pushes to the current branch. Change branches with git checkout before using this skill if you need to push to a different branch.

faq.question: What if I don't have a remote configured?
faq.answer: The push operation will fail. You need to add a remote with git remote add origin URL before using this skill.

faq.question: Does this skill handle merge conflicts?
faq.answer: No, merge conflicts must be resolved manually before using this skill. The git commit will fail if there are unresolved conflicts.

faq.question: What commit message format is used by default?
faq.answer: The default message is 'chore: update code' following conventional commit format. You can provide a custom message as a parameter.

faq.question: Does this work with GitHub, GitLab, and Bitbucket?
faq.answer: Yes, this skill uses standard git commands that work with any git remote provider including GitHub, GitLab, Bitbucket, and self-hosted git servers.
